items.title: U.S. plans evacuation for Americans on cruise ship in hantavirus outbreak

items.content: The MV Hondius is currently traveling to the Canary Islands, where the 147 people on board will be methodically off-boarded and flown home.

items.title: 7 states prepare to receive Americans possibly exposed to hantavirus

items.content: The U.S. has entered emergency response mode as a cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak sails toward one of Spain’s Canary Islands.

items.title: New plan to evacuate Americans from Hantavirus cruise
items.standfirst: The State Department is arranging a flight from the Canary Islands back to the U.S., where the 17 Americans on board the cruise ship will isolate at the national quarantine unit at Nebraska Medicine in Omaha. NBC News’ Daniele Hamamdjian reports.
items.content: The State Department is arranging a flight from the Canary Islands back to the U.S., where the 17 Americans on board the cruise ship will isolate at the national quarantine unit at Nebraska Medicine in Omaha. NBC News’ Daniele Hamamdjian reports.

items.content: Canary Island residents are concerned about the cruise ship with the hantavirus outbreak; The Pentagon releases UFO files.

items.title: Worldwide race to trace passengers from hantavirus-hit cruise ship
items.standfirst: About 12 countries have so far been linked to the hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius, as the UN health agency confirms at least five cases.
items.content: About 12 countries have so far been linked to the hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius, as the UN health agency confirms at least five cases.

items.title: Protests in the Canary Islands as virus-stricken ship heads for port

items.content: Protesters in Tenerife are voicing opposition to the imminent arrival of a cruise ship hit by hantavirus outbreak

items.title: Americans on hantavirus cruise ship reportedly to be quarantined in US
items.standfirst: US CDC personnel are reportedly meeting ship in Canary Islands to accompany Americans on a flight to NebraskaThe US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) is reportedly sending personnel to the Canary Islands to meet the cruise ship affected by the hantavirus outbreak, with plans to accompany American passengers back to the US on a chartered flight and place them into quarantine in Nebraska.An additional CDC team is already headed to Nebraska, according to unnamed sources who spoke with CNN. The sources indicated that passengers are expected to undergo quarantine measures there to help prevent any possible spread of the virus. Nebraska is home to both the federally supported National Quarantine Unit and the Nebraska Biocontainment Unit. Continue reading...
items.content: US CDC personnel are reportedly meeting ship in Canary Islands to accompany Americans on a flight to NebraskaThe US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) is reportedly sending personnel to the Canary Islands to meet the cruise ship affected by the hantavirus outbreak, with plans to accompany American passengers back to the US on a chartered flight and place them into quarantine in Nebraska.An additional CDC team is already headed to Nebraska, according to unnamed sources who spoke with CNN. The sources indicated that passengers are expected to undergo quarantine measures there to help prevent any possible spread of the virus. Nebraska is home to both the federally supported National Quarantine Unit and the Nebraska Biocontainment Unit. Continue reading...

items.title: Third British national has suspected hantavirus infection, UK government says
items.standfirst: Five cases of hantavirus have been confirmed in total, including one of the three passengers on the MV Hondius who died.
items.content: Five cases of hantavirus have been confirmed in total, including one of the three passengers on the MV Hondius who died.

items.title: French couple on hantavirus cruise ship says ‘no panic onboard’
items.standfirst: A French couple who is currently travelling on the MV Hondius, which was recently hit by a hantavirus outbreak, has issued a statement urging media not to “overdramatise” the situation, adding that there is “no panic” among the passengers onboard. "To call this an epidemic is wrong. To imply it's a pandemic is dishonest,” they said.
items.content: A French couple who is currently travelling on the MV Hondius, which was recently hit by a hantavirus outbreak, has issued a statement urging media not to “overdramatise” the situation, adding that there is “no panic” among the passengers onboard. "To call this an epidemic is wrong. To imply it's a pandemic is dishonest,” they said.

items.title: Contact tracing could be key in halting the spread of hantavirus. Here's how it works
items.standfirst: More than two dozen passengers left the cruise ship before the outbreak was identified. The race is on to connect with them to monitor their health — and the health of those they've interacted with.
items.content: More than two dozen passengers left the cruise ship before the outbreak was identified. The race is on to connect with them to monitor their health — and the health of those they've interacted with.(Image credit: lakshmiprasad S/iStockphoto)

items.title: What is hantavirus – and how worried should we be?

items.content: Three passengers have died after being on board a hantavirus-hit cruise ship that had been heading for the Canary Islands.

items.title: 17 American passengers aboard hantavirus-hit cruise ship will quarantine in Nebraska

items.content: Seventeen American passengers aboard the cruise ship at the center of a hantavirus outbreak will quarantine at a Nebraska facility that specializes in handling patients with highly communicable diseases, health officials said

items.title: U.S. departure from WHO could hinder hantavirus response
items.standfirst: The U.S. left the World Health Organization in January. Because of that, experts say, it might not get immediate access to information about the hantavirus outbreak on a Dutch-operated cruise ship.
items.content: The U.S. left the World Health Organization in January. Because of that, experts say, it might not get immediate access to information about the hantavirus outbreak on a Dutch-operated cruise ship.

items.title: How operation to disembark passengers on virus-hit cruise will work

items.content: The BBC's Guy Hedgecoe reports from Tenerife, where some locals fear the ship's arrival could bring health risks.

items.title: Contact tracing underway after passengers left ship before first reported hantavirus case
items.standfirst: The cruise ship at the center of the hantavirus outbreak is heading toward the Canary Islands in Spain. Contact tracing is underway in multiple countries including the United States after passengers left the ship before the first reported case. NBC News' Matt Bodner reports on the latest details from the outbreak.
items.content: The cruise ship at the center of the hantavirus outbreak is heading toward the Canary Islands in Spain. Contact tracing is underway in multiple countries including the United States after passengers left the ship before the first reported case. NBC News' Matt Bodner reports on the latest details from the outbreak.

clusters.title: Trump says Russia and Ukraine have agreed to his request for a 3-day ceasefire
clusters.neutral_headline: Russia and Ukraine confirm three-day ceasefire from 9 May
bullet_summary: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and Yuri Ushakov, President Vladimir Putin's foreign affairs adviser, both confirmed the agreement for a three-day ceasefire and an exchange of prisoners
The US president said on social media the ceasefire would include a suspension of all “kinetic activity” and a swap of 1,000 prisoners from each country
It was confirmed by both sides
US President Donald Trump on Friday announced a three-day ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine, starting on May 9

It was confirmed by both sides. Continue reading...

items.content: It comes as the two countries accused each other of violating separate ceasefires to cover the celebrations of the Soviet Union's victory over Nazi Germany.

items.title: Three-day ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine announced

items.content: A three-day ceasefire has been agreed by the leaders of Russia and Ukraine, as well as an exchange of prisoners, following a request from Donald Trump.

items.title: Trump announces three-day ceasefire in Russia-Ukraine war
items.standfirst: Truce will also include swap of 1,000 prisoners of war from each country, US president says.
items.content: Truce will also include swap of 1,000 prisoners of war from each country, US president says.

items.title: Trump announces three-day ceasefire between Ukraine and Russia
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump on Friday announced a three-day ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine, starting on May 9. Aside from a pause in the fighting, the two warring sides will also engage in a prisoner swap, with 1,000 prisoners from each country.
items.content: US President Donald Trump on Friday announced a three-day ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine, starting on May 9. Aside from a pause in the fighting, the two warring sides will also engage in a prisoner swap, with 1,000 prisoners from each country.

items.title: 6 videos that stood out in the Pentagon's new UFO drop
items.standfirst: The Pentagon on Friday began releasing hundreds of files on UFOs after President Trump ordered the documents to be made public. CBS News Pentagon reporter Eleanor Watson reviewed the 28 videos in the first batch of files and chose six that stood out to her the most.
items.content: The Pentagon on Friday began releasing hundreds of files on UFOs after President Trump ordered the documents to be made public. CBS News Pentagon reporter Eleanor Watson reviewed the 28 videos in the first batch of files and chose six that stood out to her the most.

items.title: Pentagon releases first batch of ‘top secret’ UFO files
items.standfirst: The Pentagon on Friday released decades of previously classified UFO sightings recorded by the FBI and NASA and other federal agencies. At least two of the more than 160 documents date back to the 1940s and report sightings of flying "discs” and “saucers”.
items.content: The Pentagon on Friday released decades of previously classified UFO sightings recorded by the FBI and NASA and other federal agencies. At least two of the more than 160 documents date back to the 1940s and report sightings of flying "discs” and “saucers”.

items.title: Substantial win for Nigel Farage's Reform UK in local elections
items.standfirst: The labor party is on the ropes in England as the results of local elections being counted show the far-right making substantial gains. The governing party has lost control of a number of councils... and pressure is piling on Keir Starmer -- with some calling for the Prime Minister to resign. Details by FRANCE 24's correspondent in London, Bénédicte Paviot.

clusters.title: South Africa’s highest court revives impeachment proceedings against the president Cyril Ramaphosa
clusters.neutral_headline: South Africa’s top court revives impeachment inquiry against president
bullet_summary: South Africa's top court has found against president Cyril Ramaphosa over the "Farmgate" scandal
Reported by France24, Al Jazeera, BBC World and others

items.title: South Africa’s highest court revives impeachment proceedings against the president Cyril Ramaphosa
items.standfirst: South Africa's President is facing the possibilty of impeachment, in a long running scandal known as Farmgate. It involves the dissapearance of over half a million dollars from Cyril Ramaposa's luxury farmhouse, which was stolen in a breakin in 2020. Ramaphosa is accused of serious violations and misconduct, and the Constitutional court has lifted an earlier ruling that blocked proceedings. He denies any wrongdoing.
items.content: South Africa's President is facing the possibilty of impeachment, in a long running scandal known as Farmgate. It involves the dissapearance of over half a million dollars from Cyril Ramaposa's luxury farmhouse, which was stolen in a breakin in 2020. Ramaphosa is accused of serious violations and misconduct, and the Constitutional court has lifted an earlier ruling that blocked proceedings. He denies any wrongdoing.

items.title: Three hikers dead after Indonesia’s Mount Dukono volcano erupts
items.standfirst: Two Singaporeans and a local person were in no-go zone when they were killed, say officialsThree hikers – two Singaporeans and an Indonesian – have died in an eruption of Indonesia’s Mount Dukono volcano, where they found themselves in a no-go zone, officials said.The eruption, on Halmahera island, sent an ash cloud about 6 miles (10km) into the air, with no towns or villages near enough to face any immediate threat. Continue reading...

items.title: Nigel Farage hails ‘historic shift in politics’ after Reform UK election gains
items.standfirst: Party has success in Labour and Tory heartland areas but one pollster says results suggest Reform may have peakedFull results from England, Scotland and WalesElections mapped: how Labour lost ground in different directionsNigel Farage hailed sweeping election wins for Reform UK as a “historic shift in British politics” on a day when the populist party made gains at the expense of Labour and the Conservatives.Reform made advances in heartland areas of both parties, clocking up substantial early results in the English local elections by taking control of Essex county council, Havering – its first London local authority – and Sunderland city council. Continue reading...

items.title: Vatican stresses need for peace as Rubio meets pope amid strained relations
items.standfirst: US secretary of state speaks with Leo and officials after tensions following attacks by Trump over Iran warThe Vatican has said it raised the “need to work tirelessly in favour of peace” in talks with the US secretary of state, Marco Rubio, who is in Rome on a trip widely regarded as an effort to ease tensions after Donald Trump’s repeated criticisms of Pope Leo.Amid unprecedented strain on relations between the Holy See and Washington, Rubio was received by the pope on Thursday at the Apostolic Palace, before holding a series of meetings with Vatican officials Continue reading...
items.content: US secretary of state speaks with Leo and officials after tensions following attacks by Trump over Iran warThe Vatican has said it raised the “need to work tirelessly in favour of peace” in talks with the US secretary of state, Marco Rubio, who is in Rome on a trip widely regarded as an effort to ease tensions after Donald Trump’s repeated criticisms of Pope Leo.Amid unprecedented strain on relations between the Holy See and Washington, Rubio was received by the pope on Thursday at the Apostolic Palace, before holding a series of meetings with Vatican officials Continue reading...

items.title: US military strike on vessel in eastern Pacific kills two people, leaving one survivor
items.standfirst: More than 190 people have been killed in such strikes on alleged drug trafficking boats in the Caribbean and PacificThe US military on Friday said it struck a vessel in the eastern Pacific, killing two people and leaving one survivor in the latest attack on boats suspected of transporting narcotics. This brings the death toll from strikes on such vessels in the Caribbean and Pacific to more than 190 people since September.A video posted by the US Southern Command shows the vessel traveling through the water being hit by what appears to be a missile. The screen momentarily goes black and then shows the boat engulfed in flames. Continue reading...

items.title: UFO files spanning decades are released by Defense Department
items.standfirst: Cold War reports of mysterious rotating saucers; recent sightings of metallic elliptical objects floating in mid-air. Those and other reports of unidentified anomalous phenomena or UAPs — the military's term for UFOs — are described in documents released Friday.
items.content: Cold War reports of mysterious rotating saucers; recent sightings of metallic elliptical objects floating in mid-air. Those and other reports of unidentified anomalous phenomena or UAPs — the military's term for UFOs — are described in documents released Friday.(Image credit: NASA)
